widelyUsed.name: Widely Used Languages
widelyUsed.description: Common languages spoken throughout the galaxy

speciesNative.name: Species Native Languages
speciesNative.description: Native languages primarily associated with specific species and homeworlds

note.localLanguages: Languages marked with * in the original list - not widely spoken except on their planet of origin or in their nomadic group.
note.rakata: Rakata was widely spoken during The Pre-Republic Era, locally spoken in all other eras.

descriptions.Aleena: Aleena is the language of the Aleena species from Aleen, a world known for quick, energetic natives and deep local traditions. It is useful in stories involving Aleena communities, racing circuits, crowded settlements, or homeworld obligations. It can sound rapid, bright, and compact, with quick emotional shifts that match the speed and restlessness often associated with Aleena speakers.
descriptions.Altirish: Altirish is a local language associated with Unknown Regions communities and is rarely encountered in the galactic core. It is useful when the campaign moves into remote settlements, nomadic routes, or GM-defined cultures beyond standard Republic maps. Its sound can be framed as regional and unfamiliar, with old local idioms that make outsiders feel far from known space.
descriptions.Anarrese: Anarrese is a local Unknown Regions language, usually relevant to remote communities rather than galactic institutions. It is useful when a character has ties to obscure frontier routes or when the GM wants language to emphasize isolation from the core. It can be portrayed as formal and insular, with pronunciations that mark speakers as outsiders to known-space society.
descriptions.Anomid: Anomid is the language of the Anomid species, famous for mask-based communication and complex social expression. It is useful in corporate, diplomatic, and cultural encounters involving Anomid enclaves or negotiators. It can sound partly vocal and partly performative, with meaning carried through tone, gesture, and the display of a specialized vocal mask.
descriptions.Anzat: Anzat is associated with the secretive Anzat and their ancient, predatory culture. It is useful for dark legends, old hunters, hidden enclaves, and investigations involving mysterious Force-sensitive predators. It should feel quiet, intimate, and unsettling, with soft phrasing that works better for secrets than public speeches.
descriptions.Anzati: Anzati is the native speech of the Anzati species and is most likely encountered in stories about ancient assassins, hunters, and hidden immortals. It is useful for decoding private warnings, old names, or lore that most spacers would rather avoid. It can sound restrained and whisper-like, with unnervingly calm delivery even when discussing violence or hunger.
descriptions.Aqualish: Aqualish is the language of the Aqualish people from Ando. It is useful in cantinas, labor crews, underworld scenes, and frontier encounters where Aqualish toughs, pilots, or workers appear. It can sound rough, barking, and confrontational, with a blunt delivery that fits hard bargains and quick tempers.
descriptions.Arkanian: Arkanian is the language of the Arkanians and Arkanian Offshoots, especially tied to Arkania's scientific and aristocratic culture. It is useful in laboratories, noble houses, medical facilities, and old Republic-era stories involving genetic engineering or elite scholarship. It can sound cold, elegant, and precise, with formal vocabulary for lineage, experimentation, and status.
descriptions.Balosar: Balosar is the language of the Balosar people. It is useful in urban underworld, narcotics, street-level intrigue, and city-planet stories where survival depends on reading social danger quickly. It can sound quick and defensive, with slang-heavy phrasing shaped by crowded streets and hard choices.
descriptions.Barabel: Barabel is the language of the Barabel species from Barab I. It is useful around hunters, warriors, wilderness guides, and communities that value direct strength and survival. It can sound harsh, sibilant, and physical, with blunt statements and threat displays woven naturally into conversation.
descriptions.Basic: Galactic Basic Standard is the everyday common tongue of the Republic, Empire, and most worlds connected to galactic trade. It is used by officials, starship crews, merchants, soldiers, and almost anyone who expects to travel beyond their homeworld. At the table, it usually sounds like plain speech in whatever accent fits the speaker, with regional quirks standing in for local dialects.
descriptions.Batorese: Batorese is the language associated with the Blood Carver people of Batorine. It is useful for assassins, ritual artisans, and mysterious alien contacts who value precision and appearance. It can sound sharp, elegant, and controlled, with dangerous calm beneath its formal phrasing.
descriptions.Besalisk: Besalisk is the language of the Besalisk species, known across the galaxy through travelers, workers, and colorful local personalities. It is useful in cantinas, repair shops, kitchens, docks, and any scene involving a gregarious Besalisk contact. It can sound booming and expressive, with overlapping gestures and hearty emphasis that makes even simple speech feel animated.
descriptions.Binary: Binary, often called Droidspeak, is a compact machine language of beeps, trills, whistles, and diagnostic tones used by astromechs and many other droids. Organic beings can learn to understand it, especially pilots, mechanics, and people who spend a lot of time with droids. It sounds musical and electronic rather than spoken, carrying emotion through pitch, rhythm, and repeated tone patterns.
descriptions.Bocce: Bocce is a practical spacer and trade language designed to bridge communication between crews, traders, port officials, and species that do not share a native tongue. It is most useful around starports, cargo yards, and frontier routes where Basic is common but not universal. It should feel clipped, modular, and borrowed from many tongues, like a working language built for deals and docking bays.
descriptions.Bothese: Bothese is the native language of the Bothans, especially associated with Bothawui, Bothan politics, and information networks. It is useful in diplomatic, intelligence, and espionage-heavy campaigns where Bothan contacts or data brokers matter. It can be portrayed as precise and layered, with careful phrasing that rewards listeners who notice implication as much as literal meaning.
descriptions.Catharese: Catharese is the language of the Cathar species and their warrior clans. It is useful in stories about Cathar heritage, clan honor, Jedi survivors, or worlds scarred by Mandalorian conflict. It can sound warm but fierce, with rolling vowels, growled emphasis, and strong terms for family and vengeance.
descriptions.Cerean: Cerean is the language of the Cerean species and their homeworld culture. It is most likely heard among Cerean communities, philosophical circles, and travelers who value careful reasoning and contemplation. It can sound measured and balanced, with a calm cadence suited to discussion, teaching, and mediation.
descriptions.Chadra-Fan: Chadra-Fan is the language of the Chadra-Fan, small bat-like beings known for heightened senses and quick emotional reactions. It is useful in technical workshops, scavenger groups, and communities where fast social cues matter. It can sound rapid, high-pitched, and chirping, with bursts of excitement that can overwhelm slower speakers.
descriptions.Cheunh: Cheunh is the language of the Chiss and is strongly associated with the Unknown Regions and Chiss society. It is useful for military, diplomatic, and intelligence campaigns involving Chiss officers, navigators, or Ascendancy interests. It can sound crisp, controlled, and formal, with precise phrasing that rewards discipline and careful etiquette.
descriptions.Chevin: Chevin is the language of the Chevin species from Vinsoth. It is useful in mercantile, political, and criminal dealings where Chevin traders or power brokers appear. It can sound slow, deep, and weighty, with careful pauses that make every bargain feel deliberate.
descriptions.Chistorian: Chistorian is the language of the Chistori species. It is useful in encounters with reptilian warriors, isolated traditions, or characters whose culture prizes physical power and discipline. It can sound deep, rasping, and territorial, with deliberate pauses that make even neutral statements feel like warnings.
descriptions.Clawdite: Clawdite is the language of the Clawdite shapeshifters of Zolan. It is useful in espionage, bounty hunting, infiltration, and identity-focused stories. It can sound flexible and performative, with a speaker's chosen persona affecting accent and rhythm as much as vocabulary.
descriptions.Codruese: Codruese is the language of the Codru-Ji and their unusual four-armed culture. It is useful in family, clan, and diplomatic stories involving Codru-Ji communities. It can sound layered and expressive, with ritual phrasing suited to kinship, negotiation, and protective social customs.
descriptions.Defel: Defel is the language of the Defel species, sometimes called wraiths because of their unusual relationship with light and shadow. It is useful for secretive scouts, spies, and remote communities where Defel contacts are present. It can sound quiet and spare, with meaning carried through subtle timing and the sense that the speaker is partly hidden even when nearby.
descriptions.Devaronese: Devaronese is the language of the Devaronian species from Devaron. It is useful among wanderers, hunters, scouts, mercenaries, and Devaronian communities spread across the spacelanes. It can sound rolling, expressive, and bold, with enough bite to suit boasts, jokes, and challenges.
descriptions.Dosh: Dosh is the language of Trandoshans from Trandosha. It is useful around bounty hunters, slavers, hunters, mercenaries, and anyone dealing with Trandoshan clans or scorekeeping traditions. It often feels harsh and predatory, with hissing consonants and clipped phrases that suit threats, boasts, and bargaining.
descriptions.Draethos: Draethos is the language of the Draethos species, known for long lives and strong scholarly or martial traditions. It is useful for historians, Jedi, warriors, and ancient records connected to Draethos culture. It can sound deep, stern, and deliberate, like a language comfortable with long memory and hard judgment.
descriptions.Dromnyr: Dromnyr is an Unknown Regions language tied to remote cultures outside the usual traffic of the core worlds. It is useful for explorers, scouts, and campaigns where ancient or isolated peoples matter. It can be treated as resonant and unfamiliar, with regional patterns that make Basic-speaking travelers feel far from home.
descriptions.Durese: Durese is the language of the Duros, one of the galaxy's great spacefaring peoples. Because Duros travelers, pilots, and navigators spread widely, Durese is useful in starports, shipping lanes, and old spacer communities. It can sound clean, technical, and efficient, like a language comfortable with navigation, machinery, and life between worlds.
descriptions.Ebruchese: Ebruchese is the language of the Ebruchi and is associated with nomadic or remote Unknown Regions groups. It is useful in frontier encounters, survival stories, and negotiations with cultures that do not rely on Republic trade norms. It can sound rough, practical, and sea-worn, like speech shaped by hard travel and clan memory.
descriptions.Ewokese: Ewokese is the language of the Ewoks of Endor's forest moon and is mostly local to their villages and hunting grounds. It is rarely useful in galactic society, but it can matter deeply in Endor stories or when dealing with scouts, shamans, and forest tribes. It sounds bright, rhythmic, and communal, with quick exclamations, chants, and expressive vocal patterns.
descriptions.Falleen: Falleen is the language of the Falleen people, a culture known for poise, aristocracy, and controlled emotional presence. It is useful in noble courts, criminal syndicates, seductive diplomacy, and encounters with Falleen leaders. It can sound smooth, restrained, and commanding, with polite phrasing that can hide threat or desire.
descriptions.Feeorin: Feeorin is the language of the Feeorin species and their rugged, long-lived culture. It is useful among pirates, mercenaries, tough spacers, and older wanderers with deep grudges. It can sound gravelly, bold, and weathered, with direct phrases that fit intimidation and hard-earned respect.
descriptions.Felucianese: Felucianese is the language of the Felucians from the fungal world of Felucia. It is useful in wilderness, Force-sensitive, tribal, and jungle survival stories set on or connected to Felucia. It can sound organic and rhythmic, with breathy calls, chants, and environmental references shaped by a vibrant living world.
descriptions.Firrerreo: Firrerreo is the language of the Firrerreo people and their scattered cultural lines. It is useful for characters with noble, refugee, or hidden heritage hooks tied to Firrerre. It can sound elegant but guarded, with formal address and emotionally controlled phrasing.
descriptions.Gamorrean: Gamorrean is the native tongue of the Gamorreans of Gamorr. It is often encountered around guards, enforcers, mercenary muscle, and Hutt-controlled criminal circles where Gamorreans serve as hired force. It sounds guttural and forceful, relying on grunts, heavy consonants, and body language as much as polite phrasing.
descriptions.Gand: Gand is the language of the Gand species and their mist-shrouded culture. It is useful for findsmen, hunters, ritual introductions, and stories where identity and humility matter. It can sound formal, nasal, and indirect, with speakers often referring to themselves by name or role rather than casual pronouns.
descriptions.Givin: Givin is the language of the Givin species of Yag'Dhul, a people famous for mathematics and vacuum survival. It is useful for astrogation, engineering, academic, and ship-design scenes involving Givin experts. It can sound exacting and strangely rhythmic, with numbers and abstractions fitting naturally into ordinary speech.
descriptions.Gran: Gran is the language of the Gran species, especially associated with Kinyen and Gran colonies. It is useful in political, mercantile, and social scenes involving Gran officials, settlers, or community leaders. It can sound social and emphatic, with careful turns of phrase used to maintain reputation and consensus.
descriptions.Gunganese: Gunganese is the language of the Gungans of Naboo, especially their underwater cities and local communities. It is useful for campaigns on Naboo or among Gungan soldiers, engineers, and civic leaders. It can sound buoyant and fluid, with rounded phrases and an energetic rhythm shaped by a culture comfortable both underwater and on land.
descriptions.High Galactic: High Galactic is a prestige language associated with courtly, noble, ceremonial, and Imperial settings. It is useful for reading old inscriptions, formal decrees, aristocratic records, and the speech of elites who want to sound elevated. It should feel polished, formal, and intentionally archaic, like a language used to create distance and authority.
descriptions.Honoghran: Honoghran is the language of the Noghri people of Honoghr. It is useful for clan loyalty, assassin traditions, covert service, and stories tied to Noghri honor. It can sound low, clipped, and disciplined, with strong ritual phrases for debt, family, and obligation.
descriptions.Houkese: Houkese is the language of the Houk species. It is useful in mercenary, labor, and rough frontier encounters where Houk strength and stubbornness matter. It can sound heavy and blunt, with short statements and forceful emphasis that leave little room for subtlety.
descriptions.Huttese: Huttese is the language of the Hutts and a major underworld lingua franca across Hutt Space, Nar Shaddaa, Tatooine, and criminal trade routes. It is useful for smugglers, bounty hunters, crime lords, gamblers, and anyone who negotiates in places where the Hutts have influence. It sounds broad, swaggering, and earthy, with phrases suited to threats, deals, insults, and indulgent boasts.
descriptions.Iktotchese: Iktotchese is the language of the Iktotchi from Iktotch. It is useful around seers, pilots, navigators, and communities where intuition and foresight are culturally respected. It can sound airy and contemplative, with long pauses that make the speaker seem to be listening to more than the room.
descriptions.Ithorese: Ithorese is the language of Ithorians, whose culture is tied to herd ships, ecological stewardship, and reverence for living worlds. It is useful when dealing with Ithorian diplomats, botanists, priests, and environmental enclaves. Because Ithorians have unusual vocal anatomy, the language can feel resonant and layered, with deep tones that seem to carry more than one pitch at a time.
descriptions.Jawa Trade Language: The Jawa Trade Language is used by Jawas when dealing with outsiders, especially in markets, salvage deals, and droid sales. It is most common on Tatooine and around Jawa sandcrawlers, but offworld scavenger clans may preserve it as a bargaining tongue. It sounds fast, squeaky, and excited, built for haggling, warning calls, and rapid appraisal of junk that might become treasure.
descriptions.Kel Dor: Kel Dor is the language of the Kel Dor people of Dorin. It is most often used among Kel Dor communities, Force traditions, and travelers tied to Dorin or Kel Dor family lines. It can sound muffled, breathy, or resonant through protective masks, giving it a controlled and deliberate quality.
descriptions.Khilese: Khilese is the language of the Khil species, known for their unusual facial hullepi and distinctive speech. It is useful in academic, corporate, and species-specific encounters involving Khil communities. It can sound airy and filtered, with soft consonants and breathy tones shaped by nonhuman vocal anatomy.
descriptions.Kiffar: Kiffar is the language of the Kiffar people of Kiffu and Kiffex. It is useful for clan politics, guardian traditions, psychometric Force themes, and stories involving Kiffar identity. It can sound grounded and declarative, with clan references and honor terms woven into everyday statements.
descriptions.Killik: Killik is the language of the insectoid Killiks and their hive-like societies. It is useful in stories about nests, joiners, ancient colonies, and collective memory. It can sound clicking, chittering, and layered, suggesting communication built as much for groups as individuals.
descriptions.Klatooinian: Klatooinian is the language of the Klatooinian species, often encountered in Hutt-influenced regions and underworld service networks. It is useful for guards, enforcers, laborers, and communities historically shaped by Hutt domination. It can sound hard, clipped, and practical, with direct phrases suited to orders, warnings, and loyalty oaths.
descriptions.Kreva: Kreva is an Unknown Regions language associated with remote peoples and isolated routes. It is useful for campaigns that push beyond familiar charts or introduce cultures only lightly touched by galactic trade. It can sound old and regional, with vocabulary that reflects local geography, clan ties, and survival needs.
descriptions.Kubaz: Kubaz is the language of the Kubaz species, known to many spacers through informants, scouts, and information brokers. It is useful for espionage, street contacts, and fringe networks where rumors travel faster than official news. It can sound nasal and buzzing, with fast phrases that feel perfect for gossip, surveillance, and coded warnings.
descriptions.Military Sign Language: Military Sign Language is a silent system of battlefield hand signals rather than a spoken tongue. It is useful for soldiers, commandos, scouts, and paramilitary teams who need to communicate without making noise. It has no sound at all, relying on clear gestures for commands like halt, danger, flank, advance, or ambush.
descriptions.Minnisiat (Trade Language): Minnisiat is a trade language used to bridge communication between different Unknown Regions groups. It is useful for merchants, scouts, and characters operating beyond well-mapped space. It can sound pragmatic and mixed, with many loanwords and simple structures designed to get strangers through business without shared culture.
descriptions.Miralukese: Miralukese is the language of the Miraluka people, a Force-sensitive species without normal sight. It is useful in Jedi, Force, spiritual, and hidden-colony stories involving perception beyond vision. It can sound calm and reverent, with metaphors of presence, aura, and inner sight replacing purely visual language.
descriptions.Mon Calamarian: Mon Calamarian is the language of the Mon Calamari of Mon Cala. It is useful around Mon Calamari shipwrights, naval officers, diplomats, and resistance cells connected to their legendary fleets. It can sound flowing and dignified, with smooth phrases that fit a people known for engineering, strategy, and oceanic culture.
descriptions.Muun: Muun is the language of the Muun species from Muunilinst, strongly associated with banking, finance, and corporate influence. It is useful in negotiations with financiers, InterGalactic Banking Clan interests, and legal or economic records. It can sound thin, precise, and highly formal, with exact terms for debt, contract, status, and obligation.
descriptions.Nikto: Nikto is the language of the Nikto species and is often encountered in Hutt-controlled territories, mercenary work, and desert or frontier communities. It is useful for dealing with Nikto guards, raiders, pilots, and clan groups. It can sound dry, forceful, and clipped, with a practical tone shaped by harsh worlds and hard service.
descriptions.Nosaurian: Nosaurian is the language of the Nosaurian species. It is useful in local politics, racing, labor, and species community scenes involving Nosaurian characters. It can sound chirping, nasal, and energetic, with quick phrases that carry frustration or excitement clearly.
descriptions.Old Corellian: Old Corellian is the ancestral language of Corellia, now mostly preserved in sayings, family traditions, songs, and old records. It is useful for Corellian heritage, smugglers with deep roots, historians, and anyone reading pre-Republic or local Corellian material. It can sound warm, rough-edged, and proud, like a language that survives in curses, ship names, and stubborn regional identity.
descriptions.Omwati: Omwati is the language of the Omwati species, known for intelligence, education, and scientific aptitude. It is useful in academic, Imperial research, and technical settings where Omwati specialists appear. It can sound precise and almost musical, with careful distinctions that reward patience and intellectual discipline.
descriptions.Ortolan: Ortolan is the language of the Ortolan species, best known through musicians, performers, and food-loving travelers. It is useful in entertainment districts, cantinas, kitchens, and communities where Ortolan performers are welcome. It can sound round, warm, and rhythmic, with a friendly musicality that makes even casual speech feel like part of a song.
descriptions.Quarrenese: Quarrenese is the language of the Quarren of Mon Cala. It is useful in underwater politics, trade disputes, separatist circles, and negotiations involving Quarren communities. It can sound nasal, sharp, and suspiciously precise, fitting a culture often portrayed as pragmatic, wary, and commercially minded.
descriptions.Quermian: Quermian is the language of the Quermian species and is most likely encountered among scholars, diplomats, and contemplative travelers. It is useful in academic or Jedi-adjacent stories involving long-lived, cerebral beings. It can sound quiet and carefully structured, with complex phrasing that mirrors a thoughtful, many-sided perspective.
descriptions.Rakata: Rakata is the language of the ancient Rakata and their Infinite Empire. It is useful for ruins, ancient technology, pre-Republic history, and dark side archaeology. It can sound severe, archaic, and ritualized, like a language meant for command, conquest, and forbidden machines.
descriptions.Rammocate (Trade Language): Rammocate is a trade language used among Unknown Regions communities that need practical communication across cultural divides. It is useful for frontier merchants, nomads, and explorers dealing with several isolated groups. It can sound utilitarian and direct, built for travel, warnings, prices, and basic diplomacy rather than poetry.
descriptions.Rodese: Rodese is the native language of Rodians and is heard wherever Rodian hunters, scouts, performers, and spacers travel. It is useful in bounty hunting circles and frontier settlements where Rodian communities have spread. It can sound reedy, quick, and chittering, with animated emphasis and sharp bursts of emotion.
descriptions.Ryl: Ryl, also known as Twi'leki, is the language of the Twi'leks of Ryloth. It is useful among Twi'lek clans, dancers, merchants, freedom fighters, nobles, and underworld circles where Twi'leks are common. It can combine spoken words with subtle head-tail and gesture cues, making it graceful, expressive, and difficult for outsiders to master fully.
descriptions.Ryn: Ryn is the language of the Ryn people, a widely scattered and often mistrusted nomadic culture. It is useful among wanderers, musicians, informants, refugees, and travelers who live between settled societies. It can sound lyrical, mobile, and coded, with songs, sayings, and private turns of phrase that preserve community memory.
descriptions.Savrip: Savrip is the language of the Mantellian Savrips. It is useful in stories involving Ord Mantell, gladiatorial creatures, wilderness threats, or misunderstood intelligent beings. It can sound deep, rumbling, and physically powerful, with simple but expressive patterns shaped by massive bodies and strong emotions.
descriptions.Selkath: Selkath is the language of the Selkath of Manaan. It is useful in Old Republic, kolto, medical, legal, and diplomatic stories tied to Manaan's neutrality. It can sound fluid and resonant, with aquatic cadence and formal restraint suited to careful negotiation.
descriptions.Shi'ido: Shi'ido is the language of the Shi'ido shapeshifters and their elusive culture. It is useful in intrigue, disguise, archival, and long-memory stories involving beings who can move through identities. It can sound fluid and adaptive, changing register easily depending on who is speaking and what role they are playing.
descriptions.Shistavanen: Shistavanen is the language of the Shistavanen wolfmen and is usually local to their homeworld or nomadic groups. It is useful for hunters, scouts, wilderness guides, and remote clans. It can sound growled, low, and alert, with short phrases designed for tracking, warning, and pack coordination.
descriptions.Shyriiwook: Shyriiwook is the best-known Wookiee language, spoken through roars, growls, barks, and resonant calls. Many non-Wookiees can learn to understand it, but most cannot physically pronounce it. It sounds powerful and emotional rather than crude, capable of carrying warmth, grief, warning, and fury through tone and volume.
descriptions.Sith: Sith is the ancient language of the Sith people and later the language of Sith culture, ritual, and dark side tradition. It is useful for tombs, holocrons, inscriptions, alchemy, and Sith lineages connected to worlds like Korriban and Ziost. It can sound severe, ceremonial, and sharp, with words that feel carved into stone rather than casually spoken.
descriptions.Sluissese: Sluissese is the language of the Sluissi, a species famous for patience, engineering, and technical skill. It is useful in shipyards, repair docks, research stations, and negotiations with meticulous engineers. It can sound slow, careful, and exact, with a preference for precision over speed.
descriptions.Snivvian: Snivvian is the language of the Snivvian species from Cadomai Prime. It is useful among artists, scouts, travelers, and Old Republic-era contacts where Snivvian culture appears. It can sound nasal and expressive, with storytelling rhythms that suit a people often portrayed as perceptive and creative.
descriptions.Socorran: Socorran is the native language of Socorro, a volcanic desert world with deep ties to smugglers and fringe culture. It is useful among pirates, smugglers, and criminals who use it as a semi-private code away from official ears. It can sound dry, clipped, and conspiratorial, well suited to quick warnings and quiet deals in shadowed cantinas.
descriptions.Squibbian: Squibbian is the language of the Squibs, who are widely associated with scavenging, bargaining, and enthusiastic trade. It is useful around salvage crews, junk dealers, smugglers, and chaotic market scenes. It can sound fast, bright, and opportunistic, packed with sales patter, jokes, and sudden changes of subject.
descriptions.Ssi-Ruuvi: Ssi-Ruuvi is the language of the Ssi-ruuk and their distant, alien civilization. It is useful in Unknown Regions campaigns, invasion plots, and encounters with cultures far outside Republic assumptions. It can sound reptilian, formal, and unsettling, with hissing tones and rigid structures that reinforce social hierarchy.
descriptions.Sullustese: Sullustese is the language of the Sullustans from volcanic Sullust. It is useful among scouts, pilots, miners, smugglers, Rebel cells, and industrial settlements with Sullustan crews. It can sound quick, nasal, and practical, with an energetic cadence suited to tunnels, cockpits, and fast tactical chatter.
descriptions.Sy Bisti: Sy Bisti is a trade language used in the Outer Rim, Wild Space, and near the Unknown Regions. It is valuable when dealing with communities that sit beyond the comfortable reach of Basic-speaking civilization. It can sound like a flexible trade pidgin, practical and adaptable, with borrowed terms for cargo, navigation, danger, and hospitality.
descriptions.Talz: Talz is the language of the Talz species from cold, remote environments. It is useful in arctic survival, tribal diplomacy, and encounters with communities far from galactic politics. It can sound low, rumbling, and breathy, shaped by endurance, simple directness, and harsh climates.
descriptions.Tibranese: Tibranese is the language of the Tibrin system and its local cultures. It is useful in aquatic, diplomatic, and regional stories tied to Tibrin or beings from that part of space. It can sound fluid and resonant, with a cadence that suggests waterborne culture and formal public speech.
descriptions.Tof: Tof is the language of the Tof people from beyond the usual known-space frontier. It is useful in stories of raiders, isolated kingdoms, and old conflicts near the Unknown Regions. It can sound booming, swaggering, and martial, with an emphasis on boasts, command, and tradition.
descriptions.Togorian: Togorian is the language of the Togorian species, a large feline people with strong hunting traditions. It is useful in clan, wilderness, mercenary, and honor-bound warrior stories. It can sound throaty, proud, and resonant, with terms that emphasize strength, territory, and loyalty.
descriptions.Togruti: Togruti is one of the languages of the Togruta of Shili and is often used alongside Basic. It is useful in Jedi, clan, hunting, and communal social contexts where Togruta identity matters. It can sound melodic and communal, with subtle social cues that reflect a culture attuned to group movement and awareness.
descriptions.Toydarian: Toydarian is the language of the Toydarian species. It is useful in salvage markets, junk shops, local commerce, and negotiations with stubborn independent traders. It can sound nasal, buzzing, and argumentative, with a cadence perfect for haggling and dismissive laughter.
descriptions.Ubeninal: Ubeninal is the language of the Ubese and their sealed, often isolationist culture. It is useful for bounty hunters, raiders, survivalists, and stories involving masked Ubese communities. It can sound muffled, filtered, and severe when spoken through helmets or respirators, adding menace to even ordinary statements.
descriptions.Utapese: Utapese is spoken by peoples of Utapau, including the Utai and other local cultures. It is useful in sinkhole cities, droid war battlefields, and regional negotiations on Utapau. It can sound clipped and echoing, with practical phrases shaped by vertical cities, labor, and survival in deep terrain.
descriptions.Vagaari: Vagaari is the language of the Vagaari, a nomadic and often hostile people of the Unknown Regions. It is useful for raider negotiations, interrogation scenes, and campaigns involving threats from beyond the mapped galaxy. It can sound harsh and militarized, with clipped orders and little patience for politeness.
descriptions.Verpine: Verpine is the language of the Verpine, insectoid engineers renowned for technology and ship design. It is useful in asteroid colonies, repair yards, research stations, and advanced technical communities. It can sound chittering and precise, with a strong technical vocabulary and rapid exchanges among experts.
descriptions.Vratix: Vratix is the language of the Vratix species, associated with insectoid society and the production culture of Thyferra. It is useful in medical supply, bacta, corporate, and hive-community stories. It can sound clicking and structured, with group-oriented phrasing and careful distinctions around labor, caste, and duty.
descriptions.Vurk: Vurk is the language of the Vurk species and their homeworld culture. It is useful when dealing with Vurk travelers, quiet observers, and communities shaped by migration or philosophical reflection. It can sound calm and deliberate, with open vowels and patient phrasing that avoids needless aggression.
descriptions.Weequay: Weequay is the language of the Weequay species, often encountered among pirates, mercenaries, sailors, and Hutt-aligned crews. It is useful in underworld campaigns, desert ports, and rough starship crews. It can sound dry, gravelly, and direct, with ritual phrases and terse social cues that outsiders can easily miss.
descriptions.Whiphid: Whiphid is the language of the Whiphid species from Toola. It is useful in cold-world survival, hunting, mercenary, and tribal encounters. It can sound growled, heavy, and breathy, with a rhythm suited to endurance, hunger, and the direct language of predators.
descriptions.Wroonian: Wroonian is the language of the blue-skinned Wroonians and their far-flung spacer culture. It is useful among merchants, explorers, scouts, and charming wanderers. It can sound lively and conversational, with a relaxed cadence that fits travelers who are comfortable making friends in strange ports.
descriptions.Yarkora: Yarkora is the language of the Yarkora species. It is useful in rare-species encounters, long-distance travel stories, and GM-defined cultural mysteries involving Yarkora communities. It can sound measured and slightly mournful, with long vowels and careful phrasing that makes speakers seem observant and patient.
descriptions.Yevethan: Yevethan is the language of the Yevetha, a fierce and insular people associated with the Koornacht Cluster. It is useful in military, political, and xenophobic isolationist storylines. It can sound hard, formal, and severe, with a tone that emphasizes purity, command, and hostility toward outsiders.
descriptions.Zabrak: Zabrak is the language of Zabrak communities from worlds such as Iridonia and Dathomir. It is useful among warrior traditions, clan groups, scouts, and Force-sensitive lineages with Zabrak roots. It can sound firm and percussive, with clipped phrasing that suggests endurance, discipline, and directness.
descriptions.Zeltron: Zeltron is the language of the Zeltron species from Zeltros. It is useful in social, diplomatic, entertainment, and pleasure-world campaigns involving charm, emotion, and persuasion. It can sound warm, expressive, and inviting, with rich emotional nuance and a tendency toward playful emphasis.
descriptions.Zolanese: Zolanese is associated with Zolan and the cultures from which Clawdites emerged. It is useful in identity, exile, shapeshifter, and homeworld stories involving Clawdite or Zolander history. It can sound formal and regionally inflected, with local expressions that distinguish homeworld identity from adopted disguises.
descriptions.Advb: Advb is used by Advozsec engineers and pessimists from Riflor. It is most often encountered around their homeworld, diaspora enclaves, military units, merchant ships, or scenes where that culture matters. At the table, it can sound like a recognizable native cadence shaped by the species’ physiology, homeworld, and history.
descriptions.Amanese: Amanese is the language of the Amanin and has no standard written form in Saga Edition species rules. It is useful in wilderness, slavery, scouting, and fringe stories involving Amanin guides or survivors. It can sound physical and direct, with simple spoken signals suited to movement, threat, and survival.
descriptions.Arconese: Arconese is used by Arcona communities. It is most often encountered around their homeworld, diaspora enclaves, military units, merchant ships, or scenes where that culture matters. At the table, it can sound like a recognizable native cadence shaped by the species’ physiology, homeworld, and history.

descriptions.Jawaese: Jawaese is the native language of the Jawas of Tatooine. It is most often heard in sandcrawlers, scavenger markets, desert trade meets, and hidden clan gatherings. It can sound fast, chittering, and nasal, with expressive bursts that make bargaining feel like a swarm of excited signals.

descriptions.Mando'a: Mando'a is the Mandalorian language of clans, warriors, adoption traditions, and military identity. It is useful around Mandalorians, mercenary companies, clan holdings, and campaigns dealing with armor, honor, and found-family loyalty. It should sound compact, direct, and battle-ready, with strong consonants and phrases that carry cultural weight.
descriptions.Massan: Massan is associated with Polis Massans, who often rely on silent or nonstandard communication. It is useful in medical facilities, archaeological stations, and stories involving Polis Massa. It should feel restrained and technical, with communication often supported by writing, gestures, or devices rather than ordinary speech.
descriptions.Mrlsstese: Mrlsstese is used by Mrlssi scholars and technical communities. It is most often encountered around their homeworld, diaspora enclaves, military units, merchant ships, or scenes where that culture matters. descriptions.Nagaian: Nagaian is used by Nagai families, duelists, and exiles. It is most often encountered around their homeworld, diaspora enclaves, military units, merchant ships, or scenes where that culture matters. descriptions.Nautila: Nautila is the Nautolan language, evolved underwater and tied strongly to pheromonal meaning. It is most useful on Glee Anselm, underwater settlements, and among Nautolans who can perceive the cues that complete the language. Out of water it loses nuance, so it may sound flowing and aquatic while relying on scent and environment for full meaning.

descriptions.Skakoform: Skakoform is the written language used by Skakoans, separate from their spoken Skakoverbal. It is most useful for technical documents, sealed-environment signage, and records tied to Skakoan industry. It should feel precise, diagrammatic, and engineered, as much a notation system as a script.
descriptions.Skakoverbal: Skakoverbal is the spoken language of Skakoans, used alongside their specialized written form, Skakoform. It is most useful in Skakoan engineering circles, pressure-suit environments, and Techno Union stories. It can sound filtered, technical, and breath-controlled, shaped by the needs of life-support gear and sealed atmospheres.
descriptions.Sriluurian: Sriluurian is used by Weequay communities from Sriluur. descriptions.Tusken: Tusken is the language of Tusken Raiders of Tatooine. It is most useful in desert survival, clan negotiations, raids, and stories involving the deep traditions of the Dune Sea. It can sound harsh, masked, and percussive, often carried through breath filters, gestures, and ritualized calls.
descriptions.Ubese: Ubese is used by Ubese mercenaries and survivors of Uba IV. descriptions.Yuzz: Yuzz is the spoken language of the Yuzzem and has no standard written form in Saga Edition species rules. It is most often heard among Yuzzem laborers, rebels, and enslaved communities resisting Imperial control. It can sound deep, forceful, and emotional, with growls and tusked articulation carrying anger, loyalty, and urgency.
